reasoning:
The FIDE top women rankings are updated on the 1st of every month, and Wikipedia reflects these updates reliably shortly after. As of November 1, 2025, Kateryna Lagno is ranked 9th exactly, with a rating of 2517. 
For 2025-11-16, the Wikipedia page will almost certainly still display the Nov 1 FIDE list, keeping her ranking at 9, which satisfies 'as high or higher than 9'. This makes the first date a near certainty.
For 2025-12-09, the Dec 1 FIDE list will be used. Lagno is only 4 points ahead of 10th ranked Bibisara Assaubayeva (2513), but she is also only 1 point behind 8th ranked Anna Muzychuk (2518), meaning standard ranking fluctuations have roughly symmetric effects. With no major women's classical tournaments prominent globally in November 2025 immediately impacting her, she has a good baseline to stay in the top 9, though fast-rising young players could over-perform (Assaubayeva or Deshmukh). I place this at 68%. 
For 2026-02-07, there will be structural updates corresponding to the December and January tournament results, bringing more overall volatility. The probability decays further toward the baseline random-walk chance of remaining in the top 9 (around 55%).
